title: Introduction to animejs
text: animejs provides R bindings Anime.js v4, JavaScript animation library. produces self-contained HTML widgets via htmlwidgets package, render browser environments like RStudio Viewer, R Markdown documents, Quarto reports, Shiny applications. package three conceptual layers: Animations – anime_animate() describes single animation; anime_timeline() anime_add() accumulate multi-segment animation specification R. Properties – anime_from_to() anime_keyframes() describe individual CSS/SVG properties move time. Rendering – anime_render() serialises specification wraps htmlwidget.

title: Introduction to animejs
text: SVG whose elements can identified valid CSS selector can targeted directly.
code: library(animejs)  svg_src <- ' <svg viewBox="0 0 400 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">   <circle data-animejs-id="c1" cx="50"  cy="100" r="20" fill="#4e79a7"/>   <circle data-animejs-id="c2" cx="120" cy="100" r="20" fill="#f28e2b"/>   <circle data-animejs-id="c3" cx="190" cy="100" r="20" fill="#e15759"/> </svg> '  anime_timeline(   duration = 800,   ease = anime_easing_elastic(),   loop = TRUE ) |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_css("circle"),     props = list(       translateY = anime_from_to(-80, 0),       opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1)     ),     stagger = anime_stagger(150, from = "first")   ) |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("c2"),     props = list(r = anime_from_to(20, 40)),     offset = "+=200"   ) |>   anime_playback(controls = TRUE) |>   anime_render(svg = svg_src, width = "360px")

title: Introduction to animejs
text: anime_animate() R equivalent Anime.js v4’s animate(targets, parameters): one set property animations applied one selector, without timeline. Single animations support playback modifiers, event callbacks, rendering options timelines.
code: anime_animate(   selector = anime_target_css("circle"),   props = list(     translateY = anime_from_to(-60, 0),     opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1)   ),   ease = anime_easing_spring(),   stagger = anime_stagger(120) ) |>   anime_render(svg = svg_src, width = "360px")

title: Introduction to animejs
text: anime_timeline() initialises animation timeline. duration, ease, delay arguments set defaults apply every segment; individual segments may override via anime_add(). anime_add() appends one segment – set property animations applied CSS selector. offset argument positions segment timeline: "+=0" (default) starts immediately previous segment; "+=200" inserts 200 ms gap.
code: tl <- anime_timeline(   duration = 1000,   ease = anime_easing("Cubic", "inOut"),   loop = TRUE )

title: Introduction to animejs
text: anime_from_to() simplest property descriptor: start value end value, optional CSS unit per-property easing override.
code: anime_from_to(0, 1) # opacity: 0 → 1 anime_from_to(0, 100, "px") # translateX: "0px" → "100px" anime_from_to(0, 1, ease = anime_easing_spring()) # per-property easing

title: Introduction to animejs
text: anime_keyframes() accepts two positional values. Bare values used values; lists may additionally specify ease, duration, delay per keyframe.
code: # Bare numeric keyframes anime_keyframes(0, 1, 0.5)  # List-based keyframes with per-keyframe easing anime_keyframes(   list(to = 0),   list(to = 1, ease = "outQuad", duration = 400),   list(to = 0.5, ease = anime_easing_bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)) )

title: Introduction to animejs
text: anime_stagger() distributes animation start times across elements matched selector. passed stagger argument anime_add() anime_animate(). start argument adds base delay staggered delays, reversed = TRUE reverses stagger order. two-dimensional grid layouts, supply grid = c(rows, cols) optionally axis = "x" axis = "y".
code: anime_add(   tl,   selector = ".bar",   props = list(scaleY = anime_from_to(0, 1)),   stagger = anime_stagger(80, from = "center") )

title: Introduction to animejs
text: easing constructors return anime_easing objects map onto Anime.js v4 equivalents: Plain Anime.js v4 easing name strings (e.g. "inOutSine", "outElastic(1,0.3)") also accepted wherever anime_easing object expected. Note Anime.js v4 removed string syntax cubic bezier, steps, spring easings – R constructors handle automatically reconstructing corresponding function calls JavaScript.

title: Introduction to animejs
text: anime_playback() sets looping, direction, speed, UI controls timeline single animation. Arguments left NULL keep settings already stored object. Setting controls = TRUE injects play/pause button scrub slider widget.
code: tl |>   anime_playback(     loop = TRUE,     loop_delay = 250,     alternate = TRUE,     playback_rate = 1.5,     controls = TRUE   ) |>   anime_render(svg = svg_src)

title: Introduction to animejs
text: anime_on() registers global JavaScript function lifecycle callback. Inject function page via htmltools::tags$script(). Valid events "onBegin", "onBeforeUpdate", "onUpdate", "onRender", "onLoop", "onPause", "onComplete", matching Anime.js v4 callback API.
code: tl |>   anime_on("onComplete", "myOnCompleteHandler") |>   anime_render(svg = svg_src)

title: Introduction to animejs
text: animejsOutput() renderAnimejs() embed widgets Shiny applications:
code: library(shiny)  ui <- fluidPage(   sliderInput("duration", "Duration (ms)", 200, 2000, 800),   animejsOutput("anim", height = "200px") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$anim <- renderAnimejs({     anime_animate(       selector = anime_target_css("circle"),       props = list(opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1)),       duration = input$duration     ) |>       anime_render(svg = svg_src)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Introduction to animejs
text: reproducible documents widget can saved self-contained HTML file:
code: widget <- anime_render(tl, svg = svg_src) htmlwidgets::saveWidget(widget, "animation.html", selfcontained = TRUE)

title: Animating the DeZIM Institut Logo
text: original SVG carries animation identifiers. code uses sub() inject data-animejs-id attributes five individually targeted elements prepend initial-state CSS rules existing <defs><style> block. Elements animated groups reached via class selectors.
code: library(animejs)  svg_raw <- paste0(   readLines("logo-dezim.svg", warn = FALSE),   collapse = "\n" )  svg_annotated <- svg_raw |>   sub(     pattern = "<defs><style>",     replacement = paste0(       "<defs><style>",       ".anim-circle{opacity:0;transform:scale(0)}",       ".anim-divider{opacity:0;transform:scaleY(0)}",       ".e{opacity:0}",       ".f,.j,.l{opacity:0}",       ".i{opacity:0;transform:translateX(10px)}"     ),     fixed = TRUE   ) |>   sub(     pattern = '<path class="d"',     replacement = '<path data-animejs-id="circle-teal" class="d anim-circle" style="transform-origin:49.5px 49.5px"',     fixed = TRUE   ) |>   sub(     pattern = '<path class="h"',     replacement = '<path data-animejs-id="circle-amber" class="h anim-circle" style="transform-origin:89.1px 49.5px"',     fixed = TRUE   ) |>   sub(     pattern = '<path class="k"',     replacement = '<path data-animejs-id="circle-red" class="k anim-circle" style="transform-origin:148.5px 49.5px"',     fixed = TRUE   ) |>   sub(     pattern = '<path class="g" d="M234',     replacement = '<path data-animejs-id="circle-orange" class="g anim-circle" style="transform-origin:218px 49.5px" d="M234',     fixed = TRUE   ) |>   sub(     pattern = '<path class="g" d="M253',     replacement = '<path data-animejs-id="divider" class="g anim-divider" style="transform-origin:253.15px 49.5px" d="M253',     fixed = TRUE   )

title: Animating the DeZIM Institut Logo
code: tl <- anime_timeline() |>   # Four circles scale in from their geometric centres, left to right.   # anime_easing_back() produces a brief overshoot that reinforces the   # circular geometry of the icon.   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("circle-teal"),     props = list(opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1), scale = anime_from_to(0, 1)),     duration = 700,     ease = anime_easing_back("out"),     offset = 0   ) |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("circle-amber"),     props = list(opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1), scale = anime_from_to(0, 1)),     duration = 700,     ease = anime_easing_back("out"),     offset = 200   ) |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("circle-red"),     props = list(opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1), scale = anime_from_to(0, 1)),     duration = 700,     ease = anime_easing_back("out"),     offset = 400   ) |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("circle-orange"),     props = list(opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1), scale = anime_from_to(0, 1)),     duration = 700,     ease = anime_easing_back("out"),     offset = 600   ) |>   # Venn lens regions fade in once all four circles are present.   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_css(".f, .j, .l"),     props = list(opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1)),     duration = 400,     ease = anime_easing("Quad", "out"),     offset = 900   ) |>   # White letterforms (D, E, Z, I, M) fade in left to right with a stagger.   # The five .e paths appear in left-to-right DOM order, so the default   # stagger direction ("first") matches the reading direction of the letters.   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_css(".e"),     props = list(opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1)),     duration = 400,     ease = anime_easing("Quad", "out"),     stagger = anime_stagger(80),     offset = 1050   ) |>   # Divider line grows from its vertical midpoint.   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("divider"),     props = list(opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1), scaleY = anime_from_to(0, 1)),     duration = 500,     ease = anime_easing("Expo", "out"),     offset = 1300   ) |>   # Text block fades in and settles leftward from a 10 px rightward offset.   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_css(".i"),     props = list(       opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1),       translateX = anime_from_to(10, 0)     ),     duration = 700,     ease = anime_easing("Quad", "out"),     offset = 1600   )

title: Animating the DeZIM Institut Logo
code: tl |>   anime_playback(autoplay = TRUE, loop = TRUE, controls = TRUE) |>   anime_render(svg_annotated, width = 520, height = 120)

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
text: complete stroke two phases. Drive (fast, ~800 ms): starting catch – knees bent, body forward, arms extended toward flywheel – rower pushes legs, swings body back, pulls handle chest. seat slides away flywheel. Recovery (slower, ~900 ms): arms extend first, body rocks forward, finally legs bend return seat catch position. animation loops two phases continuously.

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
text: machine made static SVG elements. flywheel sits left; monorail extends right; foot stretcher fixed partway along rail.
code: machine_frame <- r"(   <!-- Rail -->   <line x1="58" y1="175" x2="400" y2="175"         stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>    <!-- Rail support legs -->   <line x1="120" y1="175" x2="120" y2="192"         stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="3"/>   <line x1="340" y1="175" x2="340" y2="192"         stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="3"/>    <!-- Flywheel housing -->   <circle cx="88" cy="150" r="30"           fill="none" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="3.5"/>    <!-- Flywheel spokes -->   <line x1="88" y1="120" x2="88" y2="180"         stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/>   <line x1="58" y1="150" x2="118" y2="150"         stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/>   <line x1="67" y1="129" x2="109" y2="171"         stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/>   <line x1="109" y1="129" x2="67"  y2="171"         stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/>    <!-- Foot stretcher -->   <line x1="192" y1="157" x2="196" y2="175"         stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="3.5" stroke-linecap="round"/>   <line x1="184" y1="157" x2="200" y2="157"         stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="3.5" stroke-linecap="round"/> )"

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
text: rower elements drawn catch position. baseline animejs computes transitions; SVG coordinates encode catch geometry directly, initial CSS transform required element. rower broken six elements. carries data-animejs-id attribute R timeline can target selector produced anime_target_id().

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
text: seat rounded rectangle slides 95 px right drive (leg push moves seat away flywheel).
code: seat_svg <- r"(   <rect data-animejs-id="seat"         x="205" y="165" width="28" height="10" rx="3"         fill="#475569"/> )"

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
text: single line fixed foot (192, 175) hip catch (219, 165) represents leg. Rather fully articulated shin thigh, schematic line communicates leg extension clearly. animejs can animate SVG presentation attributes directly, hip end line animated updating x2 219 (catch) 314 (finish) seat slides.
code: leg_svg <- r"(   <line data-animejs-id="leg"         x1="192" y1="175" x2="219" y2="165"         stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="3.5" stroke-linecap="round"/> )"

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
text: torso 60 px line hip (219, 165) shoulder (193, 111). drawn, leans 26° forward vertical – catch body angle. transform-origin pinned hip CSS rotation pivots . drive hip translates 95 px right (translateX) body swings 41° clockwise (rotate), brings −26° +15° relative vertical (back lean finish). head shares pivot transform values.
code: torso_svg <- r"(   <line data-animejs-id="torso"         x1="219" y1="165" x2="193" y2="111"         stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"         style="transform-origin: 219px 165px"/> )"

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
text: head circle centred 12 px shoulder catch (190, 98). Like torso, pivots around hip (219, 165) receives identical translateX rotate values, tracks top torso exactly.
code: head_svg <- r"(   <circle data-animejs-id="head"           cx="190" cy="98" r="12"           fill="#1e293b"           style="transform-origin: 219px 165px"/> )"

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
text: arm 50 px line shoulder (193, 111) hands (150, 135), points toward flywheel handle catch. finish shoulder moved (330, 107) arms pulled straight hip level. Three transforms compose drive motion: rotate(-61) – swings arm counterclockwise flywheel-reaching angle (~151° horizontal) pointing straight (90°), pivoting catch shoulder position. translateX(137) – carries shoulder x = 193 x = 330 torso moves seat. translateY(-4) – accounts small vertical rise shoulder torso rotates back. CSS applies right left: rotate first, translateY, translateX. net effect shoulder lands precisely torso top finish.
code: arms_svg <- r"(   <line data-animejs-id="arms"         x1="193" y1="111" x2="150" y2="135"         stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="3.5" stroke-linecap="round"         style="transform-origin: 193px 111px"/> )"

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
text: handle dashed line flywheel edge (118, 150) hands (150, 135) catch. Like leg, animated via SVG attribute animation: x2 y2 track hands catch finish back.
code: handle_svg <- r"(   <line data-animejs-id="handle"         x1="118" y1="150" x2="150" y2="135"         stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="2.5"         stroke-dasharray="5 3" stroke-linecap="round"/> )"

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
code: library(animejs)  svg_src <- paste0(   '<svg viewBox="0 0 440 210" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">',   machine_frame,   seat_svg,   leg_svg,   torso_svg,   head_svg,   arms_svg,   handle_svg,   '</svg>' )

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
text: timeline two segments per loop: drive recovery. segments target six animated elements. Elements use CSS transforms (seat, torso, head, arms) targeted anime_target_id(); elements animated via SVG attributes (leg, handle) receive separate anime_add() calls x2/y2 props.

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
text: elements drawn catch require explicit value – animejs reads current state DOM. target (finish) values specified. drive uses anime_easing("Quart", "") forceful initial push decelerates toward finish.
code: tl <- anime_timeline(   duration = 800,   ease = anime_easing("Quart", "out"),   loop = TRUE ) |>   # Seat slides right   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("seat"),     props = list(translateX = 95)   ) |>   # Hip end of leg moves right with the seat   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("leg"),     props = list(x2 = 314),     offset = 0 # starts at t = 0, same as seat   ) |>   # Torso: hip moves right, body swings to back lean   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("torso"),     props = list(translateX = 95, rotate = 41),     offset = 0   ) |>   # Head: identical transforms to torso (shares hip pivot)   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("head"),     props = list(translateX = 95, rotate = 41),     offset = 0   ) |>   # Arms: rotate from flywheel-reach to pulled-in, shoulder tracks torso top   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("arms"),     props = list(       translateX = 137,       translateY = -4,       rotate = -61     ),     offset = 0   ) |>   # Handle tracks the hands   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("handle"),     props = list(x2 = 330, y2 = 156),     offset = 0   )

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
text: recovery uses anime_from_to() throughout explicitly set finish value () catch value (), since DOM now finish state. recovery easing ("inOut", "Sine") smooth symmetrical, reflecting controlled, unhurried return. recovery starts 100 ms drive ends, giving brief pause finish position.
code: tl <- tl |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("seat"),     props = list(translateX = anime_from_to(95, 0)),     duration = 900,     ease = anime_easing("Sine", "inOut"),     offset = "+=100"   ) |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("leg"),     props = list(x2 = anime_from_to(314, 219)),     duration = 900,     ease = anime_easing("Sine", "inOut"),     offset = 900   ) |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("torso"),     props = list(       translateX = anime_from_to(95, 0),       rotate = anime_from_to(41, 0)     ),     duration = 900,     ease = anime_easing("Sine", "inOut"),     offset = 900   ) |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("head"),     props = list(       translateX = anime_from_to(95, 0),       rotate = anime_from_to(41, 0)     ),     duration = 900,     ease = anime_easing("Sine", "inOut"),     offset = 900   ) |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("arms"),     props = list(       translateX = anime_from_to(137, 0),       translateY = anime_from_to(-4, 0),       rotate = anime_from_to(-61, 0)     ),     duration = 900,     ease = anime_easing("Sine", "inOut"),     offset = 900   ) |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("handle"),     props = list(       x2 = anime_from_to(330, 150),       y2 = anime_from_to(156, 135)     ),     duration = 900,     ease = anime_easing("Sine", "inOut"),     offset = 900   )

title: Animating a Rowing Stroke with animejs
code: tl |>   anime_playback(controls = TRUE) |>   anime_render(svg = svg_src, width = "360px")

what: R Bindings to the Anime.js Animation Library
title: R Bindings to the Anime.js Animation Library
text: animejs provides R bindings Anime.js v4, JavaScript animation library. produces self-contained HTML widgets via htmlwidgets render browser environments like RStudio Viewer, R Markdown documents, Quarto reports, Shiny applications.

title: R Bindings to the Anime.js Animation Library
text: can install released version animejs CRAN: install development version animejs GitHub :
code: install.packages("animejs") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("long39ng/animejs")

title: R Bindings to the Anime.js Animation Library
text: Annotate SVG elements data-animejs-id attribute CSS class, build timeline R render widget. Three circles animating view staggered elastic entrance, followed middle circle expanding.
code: library(animejs)  svg_src <- ' <svg viewBox="0 0 400 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">   <circle data-animejs-id="c1" cx="50"  cy="100" r="20" fill="#4e79a7"/>   <circle data-animejs-id="c2" cx="120" cy="100" r="20" fill="#f28e2b"/>   <circle data-animejs-id="c3" cx="190" cy="100" r="20" fill="#e15759"/> </svg> '  anime_timeline(   duration = 800,   ease = anime_easing_elastic(),   loop = TRUE ) |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_css("circle"),     props = list(       translateY = anime_from_to(-80, 0),       opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1)     ),     stagger = anime_stagger(150, from = "first")   ) |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_id("c2"),     props = list(r = anime_from_to(20, 40)),     offset = "+=200"   ) |>   anime_render(svg = svg_src)

title: R Bindings to the Anime.js Animation Library
text: Animations. anime_animate() creates single animation, mirroring Anime.js v4’s animate(). anime_timeline() initialises timeline default duration, ease, delay; anime_add() appends animation segments . Properties. anime_from_to() describes two-value transition; anime_keyframes() describes multi-step sequence. passed inside props list anime_add() anime_animate(). Stagger. anime_stagger() distributes animation start times across elements matched selector. Supports linear, centre-, 2-D grid distributions. Easing. easing constructors return anime_easing objects mapped onto Anime.js v4 equivalents. Parameterised families – anime_easing_elastic(), anime_easing_spring(), anime_easing_bezier(), anime_easing_steps(), anime_easing_back() – also available. Playback. anime_playback() controls looping, direction, speed, optional play/pause/scrub control bar injected widget. Shiny. animejsOutput() renderAnimejs() embed widgets Shiny applications.

what: Add an animation segment to a timeline — anime_add
title: Add an animation segment to a timeline — anime_add
text: Pipe-friendly. call appends one segment: set CSS property animations applied target selector.

title: Add an animation segment to a timeline — anime_add
code: anime_add(   timeline,   selector,   props,   offset = "+=0",   duration = NULL,   ease = NULL,   delay = NULL,   stagger = NULL )

title: Add an animation segment to a timeline — anime_add
text: timeline anime_timeline object. selector CSS selector string identifying SVG/HTML elements animate. Use anime_target_*() helpers construct selectors. props named list property animations. Values may scalars, two-element numeric vectors (/), anime_from_to() objects, anime_keyframes() objects. offset Timeline position. "+=N" means N ms previous segment ends; bare number absolute position ms. duration Overrides timeline default segment. ease Overrides timeline default segment. delay Overrides timeline default segment. stagger anime_stagger object per-element delay offsets.

title: Add an animation segment to a timeline — anime_add
text: modified anime_timeline object.

title: Add an animation segment to a timeline — anime_add
code: anime_timeline() |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_class("circle"),     props    = list(opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1)),     duration = 600   ) #> $defaults #> $defaults$duration #> [1] 1000 #>  #> $defaults$ease #> $name #> [1] "outQuad" #>  #> $params #> list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing" #>  #> $defaults$delay #> [1] 0 #>  #>  #> $loop #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $segments #> $segments[[1]] #> $segments[[1]]$selector #> [1] ".circle" #>  #> $segments[[1]]$props #> $segments[[1]]$props$opacity #> $from #> [1] 0 #>  #> $to #> [1] 1 #>  #> $unit #> [1] "" #>  #> $ease #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_from_to" #>  #>  #> $segments[[1]]$offset #> [1] "+=0" #>  #> $segments[[1]]$duration #> [1] 600 #>  #>  #>  #> $events #> list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_timeline"

what: Create a single Anime.js animation — anime_animate
title: Create a single Anime.js animation — anime_animate
text: R equivalent Anime.js v4's animate(targets, parameters): one set property animations applied one target selector, without timeline. Use anime_timeline() anime_add() instead several segments must sequenced.

title: Create a single Anime.js animation — anime_animate
code: anime_animate(   selector,   props,   duration = NULL,   ease = NULL,   delay = NULL,   loop = FALSE,   alternate = FALSE,   reversed = FALSE,   autoplay = TRUE,   stagger = NULL )

title: Create a single Anime.js animation — anime_animate
text: selector CSS selector string identifying SVG/HTML elements animate. Use anime_target_*() helpers construct selectors. props named list property animations. Values may scalars, two-element numeric vectors (/), anime_from_to() objects, anime_keyframes() objects. duration Duration milliseconds. NULL uses Anime.js default. ease Easing, anime_easing object Anime.js easing name string. NULL uses Anime.js default. delay Delay milliseconds animation starts. NULL uses Anime.js default. loop Logical positive integer. FALSE looping, TRUE infinite looping, fixed number iterations. alternate Logical. Alternate direction iteration. reversed Logical. Play reverse end. autoplay Logical. Start playing immediately load. stagger anime_stagger object per-element delay offsets.

title: Create a single Anime.js animation — anime_animate
text: anime_animation object.

title: Create a single Anime.js animation — anime_animate
text: Like timeline, anime_animation can modified anime_playback() anime_on(), rendered anime_render().

title: Create a single Anime.js animation — anime_animate
code: anime_animate(   selector = anime_target_class("circle"),   props = list(     translateY = anime_from_to(-40, 0),     opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1)   ),   duration = 600,   ease = anime_easing_spring() ) #> $selector #> [1] ".circle" #>  #> $props #> $props$translateY #> $from #> [1] -40 #>  #> $to #> [1] 0 #>  #> $unit #> [1] "" #>  #> $ease #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_from_to" #>  #> $props$opacity #> $from #> [1] 0 #>  #> $to #> [1] 1 #>  #> $unit #> [1] "" #>  #> $ease #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_from_to" #>  #>  #> $duration #> [1] 600 #>  #> $ease #> $name #> [1] "spring" #>  #> $params #> $params$bounce #> [1] 0.5 #>  #> $params$duration #> [1] 628 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing" #>  #> $delay #> NULL #>  #> $loop #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $alternate #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $reversed #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $autoplay #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $stagger #> NULL #>  #> $events #> list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_animation"

title: Easing constructors — anime_easing
text: family constructors Anime.js v4 easing specifications. returns anime_easing object serialises corresponding Anime.js v4 easing inside anime_timeline(), anime_add(), anime_animate(), anime_playback().

title: Easing constructors — anime_easing
code: anime_easing(family = "Quad", direction = "out")  anime_easing_elastic(direction = "out", amplitude = 1, period = 0.3)  anime_easing_back(direction = "out", overshoot = 1.70158)  anime_easing_bezier(x1, y1, x2, y2)  anime_easing_steps(count, from_start = FALSE)  anime_easing_spring(bounce = 0.5, duration = 628)

title: Easing constructors — anime_easing
text: family Character. One "linear", "Quad", "Cubic", "Quart", "Quint", "Sine", "Expo", "Circ", "Bounce". direction Character. One "", "", "inOut", "outIn". amplitude, period (Elastic easing) Numeric. Overshoot amplitude [1, 10] oscillation period (0, 2]. overshoot (Back easing) Numeric. Overshoot amount. x1, y1, x2, y2 (Cubic bezier easing) Coordinates first second control point. x1 x2 must [0, 1]. count (Steps easing) Positive integer. Number discrete steps. from_start (Steps easing) Logical. TRUE, value jumps start step instead end (CSS jump-start vs jump-end). bounce (Spring easing) Number [-1, 1]. Controls bounciness. Values 0 1 produce bouncy curves; values 0 produce -damped curves. Keep within [-0.5, 0.5] predictable behaviour. duration (Spring easing) Number [10, 10000]. perceived duration milliseconds animation feels visually complete.

title: Easing constructors — anime_easing
text: anime_easing object.

title: Easing constructors — anime_easing
text: Plain Anime.js v4 easing name strings (e.g. "inOutSine", "outElastic(1,0.3)") also accepted wherever anime_easing object expected. Note Anime.js v4 removed string syntax cubicBezier(), steps(), spring easings; use constructors , widget reconstructs corresponding function calls JavaScript.

title: Easing constructors — anime_easing
code: anime_easing("linear") #> $name #> [1] "linear" #>  #> $params #> list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing" anime_easing("Quad", "outIn") #> $name #> [1] "outInQuad" #>  #> $params #> list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing"  anime_easing_elastic() #> $name #> [1] "outElastic" #>  #> $params #> $params$amplitude #> [1] 1 #>  #> $params$period #> [1] 0.3 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing" anime_easing_elastic("in", amplitude = 1.5, period = 0.3) #> $name #> [1] "inElastic" #>  #> $params #> $params$amplitude #> [1] 1.5 #>  #> $params$period #> [1] 0.3 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing"  anime_easing_back() #> $name #> [1] "outBack" #>  #> $params #> $params$overshoot #> [1] 1.70158 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing" anime_easing_back("in", overshoot = 2.5) #> $name #> [1] "inBack" #>  #> $params #> $params$overshoot #> [1] 2.5 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing"  anime_easing_bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) #> $name #> [1] "cubicBezier" #>  #> $params #> $params$x1 #> [1] 0.4 #>  #> $params$y1 #> [1] 0 #>  #> $params$x2 #> [1] 0.2 #>  #> $params$y2 #> [1] 1 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing" anime_easing_bezier(0.68, -0.55, 0.265, 1.55) #> $name #> [1] "cubicBezier" #>  #> $params #> $params$x1 #> [1] 0.68 #>  #> $params$y1 #> [1] -0.55 #>  #> $params$x2 #> [1] 0.265 #>  #> $params$y2 #> [1] 1.55 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing"  anime_easing_steps(10) #> $name #> [1] "steps" #>  #> $params #> $params$count #> [1] 10 #>  #> $params$from_start #> [1] FALSE #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing" anime_easing_steps(5, from_start = TRUE) #> $name #> [1] "steps" #>  #> $params #> $params$count #> [1] 5 #>  #> $params$from_start #> [1] TRUE #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing"  anime_easing_spring() #> $name #> [1] "spring" #>  #> $params #> $params$bounce #> [1] 0.5 #>  #> $params$duration #> [1] 628 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing" anime_easing_spring(bounce = 0.65, duration = 350) #> $name #> [1] "spring" #>  #> $params #> $params$bounce #> [1] 0.65 #>  #> $params$duration #> [1] 350 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing"

what: Specify a from/to property range — anime_from_to

title: Specify a from/to property range — anime_from_to
text: Numeric. Starting value. Numeric. Ending value. unit Character. Optional CSS unit suffix, e.g. "px", "%", "deg". ease Optional easing override property alone, anime_easing object Anime.js easing name string.

code: anime_from_to(0, 1) #> $from #> [1] 0 #>  #> $to #> [1] 1 #>  #> $unit #> [1] "" #>  #> $ease #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_from_to" anime_from_to(0, 360, unit = "deg") #> $from #> [1] 0 #>  #> $to #> [1] 360 #>  #> $unit #> [1] "deg" #>  #> $ease #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_from_to" anime_from_to(0, 1, ease = anime_easing_spring()) #> $from #> [1] 0 #>  #> $to #> [1] 1 #>  #> $unit #> [1] "" #>  #> $ease #> $name #> [1] "spring" #>  #> $params #> $params$bounce #> [1] 0.5 #>  #> $params$duration #> [1] 628 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing" #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_from_to"

code: anime_keyframes(...)

text: anime_keyframes object.

title: Specify per-property keyframes for an animation — anime_keyframes
code: # Bare numeric keyframe values anime_add(   anime_timeline(),   selector = ".circle",   props = list(     opacity = anime_keyframes(0, 1, 0.5),     translateY = anime_keyframes(-20, 0, 10)   ) ) #> $defaults #> $defaults$duration #> [1] 1000 #>  #> $defaults$ease #> $name #> [1] "outQuad" #>  #> $params #> list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing" #>  #> $defaults$delay #> [1] 0 #>  #>  #> $loop #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $segments #> $segments[[1]] #> $segments[[1]]$selector #> [1] ".circle" #>  #> $segments[[1]]$props #> $segments[[1]]$props$opacity #> [[1]] #> [1] 0 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[3]] #> [1] 0.5 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_keyframes" #>  #> $segments[[1]]$props$translateY #> [[1]] #> [1] -20 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 0 #>  #> [[3]] #> [1] 10 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_keyframes" #>  #>  #> $segments[[1]]$offset #> [1] "+=0" #>  #>  #>  #> $events #> list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_timeline"  # Per-keyframe lists with optional ease and duration overrides anime_add(   anime_timeline(),   selector = ".circle",   props = list(     opacity = anime_keyframes(       list(to = 0),       list(to = 1, ease = anime_easing("Cubic"), duration = 400),       list(to = 0.5, ease = "linear", duration = 200)     )   ) ) #> $defaults #> $defaults$duration #> [1] 1000 #>  #> $defaults$ease #> $name #> [1] "outQuad" #>  #> $params #> list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing" #>  #> $defaults$delay #> [1] 0 #>  #>  #> $loop #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $segments #> $segments[[1]] #> $segments[[1]]$selector #> [1] ".circle" #>  #> $segments[[1]]$props #> $segments[[1]]$props$opacity #> [[1]] #> [[1]]$to #> [1] 0 #>  #>  #> [[2]] #> [[2]]$to #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]]$ease #> $name #> [1] "outCubic" #>  #> $params #> list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing" #>  #> [[2]]$duration #> [1] 400 #>  #>  #> [[3]] #> [[3]]$to #> [1] 0.5 #>  #> [[3]]$ease #> [1] "linear" #>  #> [[3]]$duration #> [1] 200 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_keyframes" #>  #>  #> $segments[[1]]$offset #> [1] "+=0" #>  #>  #>  #> $events #> list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_timeline"

what: Attach a JavaScript callback to an animation event — anime_on
title: Attach a JavaScript callback to an animation event — anime_on
text: callback must name globally scoped JavaScript function already present page, example one injected via htmltools::tags$script(). render time JavaScript binding resolves name window[callback] attaches corresponding Anime.js callback.

title: Attach a JavaScript callback to an animation event — anime_on
code: anime_on(x, event, callback)

title: Attach a JavaScript callback to an animation event — anime_on
text: x anime_timeline anime_animation object. event One "onBegin", "onBeforeUpdate", "onUpdate", "onRender", "onLoop", "onPause", "onComplete", matching Anime.js v4 callback API. callback Character scalar. Name global JS function invoke.

title: Attach a JavaScript callback to an animation event — anime_on
text: modified object.

title: Attach a JavaScript callback to an animation event — anime_on
code: if (interactive() && rlang::is_installed("htmltools")) {   svg_src <- '<svg viewBox="0 0 200 100" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">     <circle class="circle" cx="100" cy="50" r="20" fill="#4e79a7"/>   </svg>'    widget <- anime_timeline(duration = 800) |>     anime_add(selector = ".circle", props = list(opacity = c(0, 1))) |>     anime_on("onComplete", "handleAnimationDone") |>     anime_render(svg = svg_src)    callback_js <- htmltools::tags$script(     "function handleAnimationDone() {       console.log('Animation complete.');     }"   )    htmltools::browsable(htmltools::tagList(callback_js, widget)) }

title: Configure animation playback — anime_playback
text: Sets autoplay, looping, direction, speed, optional controls UI anime_timeline anime_animation. Arguments left NULL keep settings already stored object.

title: Configure animation playback — anime_playback
code: anime_playback(   x,   autoplay = NULL,   loop = NULL,   loop_delay = NULL,   playback_rate = NULL,   reversed = NULL,   alternate = NULL,   controls = NULL )

title: Configure animation playback — anime_playback
text: x anime_timeline anime_animation object. autoplay Logical. Start playing immediately load. loop Logical positive integer. FALSE looping, TRUE infinite looping, fixed number iterations. loop_delay Numeric. Delay milliseconds iterations. playback_rate Numeric. Playback speed multiplier (1 normal speed). reversed Logical. Play reverse end. alternate Logical. Alternate direction iteration (requires loop TRUE positive integer visible effect). controls Logical. Inject play/pause/seek control bar widget container.

title: Configure animation playback — anime_playback
text: modified anime_timeline anime_animation object.

title: Configure animation playback — anime_playback
code: anime_timeline() |>   anime_playback(loop = TRUE, alternate = TRUE, controls = TRUE) #> $defaults #> $defaults$duration #> [1] 1000 #>  #> $defaults$ease #> $name #> [1] "outQuad" #>  #> $params #> list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_easing" #>  #> $defaults$delay #> [1] 0 #>  #>  #> $loop #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $segments #> list() #>  #> $events #> list() #>  #> $alternate #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $controls #> [1] TRUE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_timeline"

what: Render an animation or timeline as an htmlwidget — anime_render
title: Render an animation or timeline as an htmlwidget — anime_render
text: Serialises anime_timeline() anime_animate() object JSON wraps together SVG payload htmlwidget.

title: Render an animation or timeline as an htmlwidget — anime_render
code: anime_render(x, svg = NULL, width = NULL, height = NULL, elementId = NULL)

title: Render an animation or timeline as an htmlwidget — anime_render
text: x anime_timeline object produced anime_timeline(), anime_animation object produced anime_animate(). svg Character. Raw SVG markup embed widget. NULL, empty string used (animation run existing DOM content – advanced use ). width Fixed width widget (css units). default NULL, results intelligent automatic sizing based widget's container. height Fixed height widget (css units). default NULL, results intelligent automatic sizing based widget's container. elementId Use explicit element ID widget (rather automatically generated one). Useful JavaScript needs explicitly discover interact specific widget instance.

title: Render an animation or timeline as an htmlwidget — anime_render
text: object class c("animejs", "htmlwidget").

title: Render an animation or timeline as an htmlwidget — anime_render
code: tl <- anime_timeline(duration = 800) |>   anime_add(     selector = anime_target_class("dot"),     props = list(opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1))   ) svg <- '<svg viewBox="0 0 100 100"><circle class="dot" cx="50" cy="50" r="10"/></svg>' if (interactive()) {   anime_render(tl, svg) }

what: Create a stagger configuration for per-element delay offsets — anime_stagger
title: Create a stagger configuration for per-element delay offsets — anime_stagger
text: applied multi-element selector, Anime.js distributes animation start times across elements according stagger value.

title: Create a stagger configuration for per-element delay offsets — anime_stagger
code: anime_stagger(   value,   from = "first",   start = NULL,   reversed = FALSE,   grid = NULL,   axis = NULL,   ease = NULL )

title: Create a stagger configuration for per-element delay offsets — anime_stagger
text: value Numeric. Base delay milliseconds element. One "first", "last", "center", numeric index. Controls element starts first. start Numeric. Starting value added every staggered delay. reversed Logical. Reverse stagger order. grid Integer vector length 2 (c(rows, cols)) 2D grid stagger. axis One "x", "y". Used together grid. ease Easing applied stagger distribution , anime_easing object Anime.js easing name string.

title: Create a stagger configuration for per-element delay offsets — anime_stagger
text: anime_stagger object.

title: Create a stagger configuration for per-element delay offsets — anime_stagger
code: # Simple linear stagger, 100 ms between elements anime_stagger(100) #> $value #> [1] 100 #>  #> $from #> [1] "first" #>  #> $start #> NULL #>  #> $reversed #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $grid #> NULL #>  #> $axis #> NULL #>  #> $ease #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_stagger"  # Stagger from centre outward anime_stagger(200, from = "center") #> $value #> [1] 200 #>  #> $from #> [1] "center" #>  #> $start #> NULL #>  #> $reversed #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $grid #> NULL #>  #> $axis #> NULL #>  #> $ease #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_stagger"  # 2-D grid stagger along the x axis anime_stagger(50, grid = c(3, 4), axis = "x") #> $value #> [1] 50 #>  #> $from #> [1] "first" #>  #> $start #> NULL #>  #> $reversed #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $grid #> [1] 3 4 #>  #> $axis #> [1] "x" #>  #> $ease #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "anime_stagger"

what: animejs: R Bindings to the Anime.js Animation Library — animejs-package
title: animejs: R Bindings to the Anime.js Animation Library — animejs-package
text: animejs provides pipe-friendly R interface Anime.js v4, JavaScript animation library. Animations authored R serialised JSON; htmlwidgets infrastructure renders browser environment. central workflow : Create single animation anime_animate(), timeline anime_timeline() add segments anime_add(). Configure playback anime_playback() attach event callbacks anime_on(). Render htmlwidget anime_render(). Property values specified via anime_from_to() anime_keyframes(). Per-element delay offsets specified via anime_stagger(). Easing functions specified via anime_easing_*() family. Target selectors constructed via anime_target_*() family. Shiny applications, use animejsOutput() renderAnimejs().

title: animejs: R Bindings to the Anime.js Animation Library — animejs-package
text: None currently. configuration per-animation.

title: animejs: R Bindings to the Anime.js Animation Library — animejs-package
text: Maintainer: Long Nguyen nguyen@dezim-institut.de (ORCID) Authors: Long Nguyen nguyen@dezim-institut.de (ORCID)

title: Shiny bindings for animejs — animejs-shiny
text: Output render functions using animejs widgets within Shiny applications interactive R Markdown documents.

title: Shiny bindings for animejs — animejs-shiny
code: animejsOutput(outputId, width = "100%", height = "400px")  renderAnimejs(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for animejs — animejs-shiny
text: outputId Output variable read . width, height Must valid CSS unit (like "100%", "400px", "auto") number, coerced string "px" appended. expr expression generates animejs widget, typically call anime_render(). env environment evaluate expr. quoted expr quoted expression (quote())? useful want save expression variable.

title: Shiny bindings for animejs — animejs-shiny
text: animejsOutput() returns Shiny output function can used UI definition; renderAnimejs() returns Shiny render function can assigned output slot.

title: Shiny bindings for animejs — animejs-shiny
code: if (interactive() && rlang::is_installed("shiny")) {   library(shiny)    svg_src <- '<svg viewBox="0 0 200 100" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">     <circle class="dot" cx="100" cy="50" r="20" fill="#4e79a7"/>   </svg>'    ui <- fluidPage(     sliderInput("duration", "Duration (ms)", 200, 2000, 800),     animejsOutput("anim", height = "200px")   )    server <- function(input, output, session) {     output$anim <- renderAnimejs({       anime_animate(         selector = anime_target_class("dot"),         props = list(opacity = anime_from_to(0, 1)),         duration = input$duration       ) |>         anime_render(svg = svg_src)     })   }    shinyApp(ui, server) }

what: Create a bare animejs htmlwidget — animejs_widget
title: Create a bare animejs htmlwidget — animejs_widget
text: low-level constructor. users call directly; final rendering step called anime_render().

title: Create a bare animejs htmlwidget — animejs_widget
code: animejs_widget(svg, config, width = NULL, height = NULL, elementId = NULL)

title: Create a bare animejs htmlwidget — animejs_widget
text: svg Character. Raw SVG markup embed widget. NULL, empty string used (animation run existing DOM content – advanced use ). config List. serialisable animation specification produced anime_timeline() anime_animate() modifiers, serialised anime_render(). width Fixed width widget (css units). default NULL, results intelligent automatic sizing based widget's container. height Fixed height widget (css units). default NULL, results intelligent automatic sizing based widget's container. elementId Use explicit element ID widget (rather automatically generated one). Useful JavaScript needs explicitly discover interact specific widget instance.

title: Create a bare animejs htmlwidget — animejs_widget
text: object class c("animejs", "htmlwidget").

title: animejs 1.0.0
text: easing serialisation protocol now matches Anime.js v4 API. Anime.js v4 removed string syntax cubic bezier, steps, spring easings, now serialised structured payloads reconstructed anime.cubicBezier(), anime.steps(), anime.spring() calls JavaScript. Spring parameters (previously silently ignored) now actually take effect. anime_on(), anime_playback(), anime_render() now take x name first argument (previously timeline), since also accept new anime_animation objects. anime_playback() arguments now default NULL, meaning “leave current setting unchanged”, instead overwriting playback settings every call. anime_target_class() anime_target_id() now require single string; anime_target_class() rejects class names leading dot instead silently producing broken selector. animejs_widget() argument timeline_config renamed config.

title: animejs 1.0.0
text: anime_animate() creates single animation without timeline, mirroring Anime.js v4’s animate(). works anime_playback(), anime_on(), anime_render(). anime_easing_steps() gains from_start argument jump start step (CSS jump-start behaviour). anime_from_to() gains ease argument per-property easing. anime_on() supports seven Anime.js v4 callbacks, adding "onBeforeUpdate", "onRender", "onPause". anime_playback() gains loop_delay playback_rate arguments. anime_stagger() gains start reversed arguments. animejsOutput() renderAnimejs() provide Shiny bindings.

title: animejs 1.0.0
text: user-facing functions now validate inputs raise structured, informative error messages. Parameterised elastic back easings longer trigger spurious browser console warnings. Easing specifications inside keyframes (anime_keyframes()) staggers (anime_stagger(ease = )) now serialised resolved correctly; previously dropped Anime.js. anime_from_to() longer converts numeric values strings unit NULL. anime_playback(controls = TRUE) longer overwrites onUpdate onComplete callbacks registered anime_on(); controls bar now chains .

title: animejs 0.1.1
text: Fix icons play/pause button

title: animejs 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-26 Initial CRAN submission.
